summary: A spirited East‑Coast 1971 performance with a blazing Dark Star/Me & My Uncle jam, but the recording suffers from odd stereo imaging and needs mastering.
review: The show captures the Dead in an inspired early‑'70s groove – highlights include a wild Dark Star → Me & My Uncle → Dark Star transition into Sitting on Top of the World and a fiery Truckin'. The source is decent, though reviewers note weird stereo spread and a need for better mastering to clean up the mix.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviewers are enthusiastic about the musical performance (5‑star praise) while noting only moderate recording flaws; weighting favors the strong show, yielding a solid >4 rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Mix has strange stereo imaging and could benefit from mastering and mid‑range correction; overall listenable but not pristine.

standout_songs: Sitting on Top of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows smoothly with extended jams, especially the Dark Star → Me & My Uncle sequence that evolves into a rare Sitting on Top of the World jam; overall energy is high and festive.

band_member_comments.Bob: Weir and Pig (Bob) are praised for their smoky performance on the NFA segment.
